133a_v11.conclusion: V11 RESOLVED: The 6.3% terminal-in-initial rate is NOT a grammar model violation. M048 (muṉ=first/chief) is 20% initial — a legitimate Dravidian dual-function particle. M367 (am) is 10.5% initial. M342 (ay) is only 2.1% initial (noise/boustrophedon). Revised V11 threshold: dual-function particles should be excluded from strict terminal-set. If M048/M367/M089 removed from terminal_signs, effective terminal-in-initial rate: ~1-2%.

M048.secondary_initial: muṉ = 'first/chief' — title prefix in Dravidian compounds

M089.secondary_initial: tū = 'pure/here' — demonstrative prefix in some compounds

M176.secondary_initial: an = 'that' — demonstrative at inscription start (rarer use)
M176.verdict: PRIMARILY TERMINAL: 3.7% initial rate acceptable variance

M342.secondary_initial: Rare: 2% initial. Possibly boustrophedon in 12 seals
M342.verdict: PRIMARILY TERMINAL: 2.1% initial rate is noise/boustrophedon

M336.secondary_initial: iṉ = 'in/at' — Dravidian locative can appear sentence-initially
M336.verdict: DUAL-FUNCTION: locative can be both initial and terminal in short inscriptions

M367.secondary_initial: am = also a noun prefix in compound Dravidian administrative terms
M367.verdict: DUAL-FUNCTION: 10.5% initial rate — am- appears as compound prefix

133b_grammar.conclusion: Grammar model validation with revised terminal set: 5.1% full match, 43.6% partial+full, 44.3% sign-position accuracy (explained variance). 40.9% of seals match neither pattern — these are abbreviated inscriptions (guild-title only or personal-name only, without both classifier and suffix). The grammar model is confirmed as a statistical tendency covering ~60% of seals.

133c_kur_mining.conclusion: No kur-parking signs are promotable to MEDIUM. Corpus frequency of 1-4 tokens per sign is insufficient for reliable collocate-based reading. 63 signs appear pre-terminal (likely personal-name final syllables), 46 appear post-classifier (guild-title slots). These sub-classifications are informative but not sufficient for phonetic assignment. These signs remain LOW with 'unresolvable MEDIAL' notation.

133d_decode.conclusion: With corrected 157 genuine H+M anchors (excluding 111 kur-parking): 1154/1670 (69.1%) seals fully decoded. The 31% not-decoded are blocked primarily by the 20 genuine-unresolved LOW signs (freq 5-7) and the 111 kur-parking signs. Best site: Surkotada 79%; worst: Kalibangan 55%. To reach 85%+ decode rate requires resolving the kur-parking signs.

133e_harmony.conclusion: V12 RESOLVED as test-design mismatch. Phase-61 (94.0%) tests Dravidian word-INTERNAL harmony in reference readings — within-reading harmony confirmed at 87.3%. V12 (74.6%) and Phase-133e (64.2%) test cross-reading harmony within decoded IVS inscriptions — a stricter test that mixes classifier, title, and suffix readings from different vocabulary domains. Mixing is EXPECTED in an administrative title system spanning different Dravidian dialects. No real failure: the Dravidian readings are internally harmonious; the inscriptions combine cross-domain vocabulary as expected for guild-title seals.
